title: oxytropis middendorffii subsp. orulganica
intro: Oxytropis middendorffii subsp. orulganica, also known as the Orulgan milkvetch, is a plant species within the Fabaceae family. It is a type of perennial legume that grows in mountainous regions of Asia, characterized by its red flowers and hairy stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. The herbal preparations of Oxytropis middendorffii subsp. orulganica are often used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and pneumonia, and to alleviate symptoms of colds and fever.
